user_query:
I need you to triage a suspected Windows host compromise using the provided memory-analysis artifacts and produce a verified incident package.

Read these files from input/:
- input/memory_pslist.csv
- input/memory_netscan.csv
- input/memory_malfind.txt
- input/yara_hits.json
- input/case_brief.md

Then deliver all of the following under output/:
1) output/report/incident_report.md — A verification-heavy, multi-stage analysis write-up with these clearly labeled sections: "Hypotheses", "Analysis", "Audit", "Adversarial Verification", "Synthesis", and "Indicators of Compromise (IOCs)". Anchor claims to evidence from the input files (cite filenames and relevant IDs/lines where possible) and provide a final risk assessment and recommended next steps.
2) output/findings/suspicious_processes.csv — A table of suspicious or notable processes with header columns exactly: pid, name, detection_sources, has_injection, network_endpoints_count, notes. Use semicolon-delimited lists in detection_sources and set has_injection as a boolean. Include at least 3 rows.
3) output/iocs/indicators.json — A JSON array of at least 12 unique IOCs with objects containing keys: type, value, source, confidence. Include a variety such as domains, IPs, file hashes, registry keys or mutexes, and file paths.
4) output/seed/mock_users.csv — Generate 50 realistic mock user profiles plausibly affected by the incident with columns: id, name, email, phone, address, company, created_at, active.
5) output/notes/export.json — Create an investigation notes export as a JSON array of objects with keys: type, time, value. Use timestamps in "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" format and include at least these types: add, plan, track, review, streak, remind, prioritize, tag, timeline, report, weekly-review.

Use only relative workspace paths. All deliverables must be self-contained text-based files with the exact requested formats.
